zh.lifeSummary: 當Princess of Volinsk Przybysława Yaroslavna Rurik於大約1117年出生時，她的父親Yaroslav Svyatopolkovich of Kiev Prince of Volynsk48歲，母親Agnes von Gandersheim Äbtissin der Stifte Gandersheim und Quedlinburg28歲。她於1134年與Racibor Gryfita I結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1156年之後在德國海因里希_包莫可萊斯 史拉維，埋葬在德國梅可倫波-沃爾包莫奧斯特沃爾包莫武多莫 文可爾。

nameMeanings.name: Rurik
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Ukrainian and Rusyn (standard transliteration Buryk): nickname derived from buryy ‘dark brown’.

Slovak (mainly Burík and Búrik) and Rusyn (from Slovakia; also Burík): nickname from the archaic Slovak name burík for a (dark) gray horse, or derived directly from an adjective meaning ‘(dark) gray’ (in Slovak) or ‘dark brown’ (in Rusyn; compare 1 above).
Czech (Buřík): variant of Buřič (see Buric 1). Compare Burick .

variants.name: Buric
meanings: Czech (Buřič) and Slovak (Burič): nickname from Czech buřič, Slovak burič ‘rebel’.

Serbian and Croatian (Burić): patronymic from Buro, which may be a short form of the old personal name Burislav (composed of the elements bur, from buriti se ‘to stir up’, + slav, from slava ‘glory’), or a nickname for a big-bellied man derived from the root of burav ‘bellied’.
Croatian (Burić): nickname derived from bura ‘north wind’ (see Bura 2) and given to a child born when this wind was blowing. Compare Burich and Burick .

variants.name: Burick
meanings: Americanized form of Ukrainian, Rusyn, and Slovak Burik or Czech Buřik.
Americanized form of Czech Buřič and Slovak Burič or Serbian and Croatian Burić (see Buric ).

en.lifeSummary: When Princess of Volinsk Przybysława Yaroslavna Rurik was born about 1117, her father, Yaroslav Svyatopolkovich of Kiev Prince of Volynsk, was 48 and her mother, Agnes von Gandersheim Äbtissin der Stifte Gandersheim und Quedlinburg, was 28. She married Racibor Gryfita I in 1134. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died after 1156, in Schlawe, Pomerania, Prussia, Germany, and was buried in Usedomer Winkel, Ostvorpommern, Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Princess of Volinsk Przybysława Yaroslavna Rurik est née environ 1117, son père, Yaroslav Svyatopolkovich of Kiev Prince of Volynsk, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Agnes von Gandersheim Äbtissin der Stifte Gandersheim und Quedlinburg, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Racibor Gryfita I en 1134. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 1156, à Kreis Schlawe, Pommern, Prusse, Allemagne, et a été enterrée à Usedomer Winkel, Ostvorpommern, Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, Allemagne.

de.Gandersheim, Northeim, Niedersachsen, Ge: Bad Gandersheim, Landkreis Northeim, Niedersachsen, Deutschland
de.lifeSummary: Als Princess of Volinsk Przybysława Yaroslavna Rurik um 1117 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Yaroslav Svyatopolkovich of Kiev Prince of Volynsk 48 und ihre Mutter Agnes von Gandersheim Äbtissin der Stifte Gandersheim und Quedlinburg 28. Sie hat 1134 Racibor Gryfita I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1156 in Kreis Schlawe, Pommern, Preußen, Deutschland und wurde in Usedomer Winkel, Ostvorpommern, Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Princess of Volinsk Przybysława Yaroslavna Rurik è nata il circa 1117, sua padre, Yaroslav Svyatopolkovich of Kiev Prince of Volynsk, aveva 48 anni e sua madre, Agnes von Gandersheim Äbtissin der Stifte Gandersheim und Quedlinburg, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Racibor Gryfita I nel 1134. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1156, nel Kreis Schlawe, Pommern, Preußen, Germania, e fu sepolta a Usedomer Winkel, Ostvorpommern, Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Princess of Volinsk Przybysława Yaroslavna Rurikが約1117年に生まれた時，父Yaroslav Svyatopolkovich of Kiev Prince of Volynskは48歳，母Agnes von Gandersheim Äbtissin der Stifte Gandersheim und Quedlinburgは28歳であった。1134年にRacibor Gryfita Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1156年以後にドイツプレウssンポンメルンクレイス スチラウで死去し，ドイツメックレンブルグ-ボルプオンメルンオストボルプオンメルンウセドマー ウィンケルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Princess of Volinsk Przybysława Yaroslavna Rurik(이)가 대략 1117년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Yaroslav Svyatopolkovich of Kiev Prince of Volynsk(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes von Gandersheim Äbtissin der Stifte Gandersheim und Quedlinburg(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1134년 Racibor Gryfita I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1156년 이후 독일 프레우ss 폼머 크레이스 슐로에서 사망했으며 독일 클렌버그-보르폼머 오스트보르폼머 세돔어 윈클에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Princess of Volinsk Przybysława Yaroslavna Rurik nasceu aproximadamente 1117, seu pai, Yaroslav Svyatopolkovich of Kiev Prince of Volynsk, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Agnes von Gandersheim Äbtissin der Stifte Gandersheim und Quedlinburg, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Racibor Gryfita I em 1134. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 1156, em Kreis Schlawe, Pommern, Preußen, Alemanha, e foi sepultada em Usedomer Winkel, Ostvorpommern, Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, Alemanha.

ru.Gandersheim, Northeim, Niedersachsen, Ge: бад гандиршим, ландкрис нортим, ниедирсачсен, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Princess of Volinsk Przybysława Yaroslavna Rurik родилась около 1117 г., её отцу по имени Yaroslav Svyatopolkovich of Kiev Prince of Volynsk было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes von Gandersheim Äbtissin der Stifte Gandersheim und Quedlinburg, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Racibor Gryfita I в 1134 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла после 1156 г. в местности крис щлав, поммирн, Пруссия, Германия и была похоронена в местности уседомир винкел, остворпоммирн, меккленбург-ворпоммирн, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Princess of Volinsk Przybysława Yaroslavna Rurik nació aproximadamente 1117, su papá, Yaroslav Svyatopolkovich of Kiev Prince of Volynsk, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Agnes von Gandersheim Äbtissin der Stifte Gandersheim und Quedlinburg, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Racibor Gryfita I en 1134. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió después de 1156, en Kreis Schlawe, Pommern, Preußen, Alemania, y fue sepultada en Usedomer Winkel, Ostvorpommern, Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, Alemania.
